schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: The heartbeat — the most basic mechanism in Cairn: ONE daemon that provides a pulse, and nothing more. It does not execute, resolve, schedule, or route. A device subscribes to the beat by placing a ticket-file in its own folder tree; the ground loop looks for those files and pulses each subscribed device's SHIM. Handling a pulse — reading the device's tickets, deciding which are due now, firing the due ones by their tag — lives in the shim (`cairn/base/shim.py`), NOT here and not in the device body. The ground loop only beats.
why: A single daemon structure that everyone else hangs their own handlers on — so no device runs a bespoke loop or daemon of its own (the UU disease: dozens of independent daemons rotting silently). One heartbeat; every device's shim hangs a list of tickets on it. The decisive property, and the reason the ground loop stays this small: the TICKET IS THE SAME UNIT whether it is fired by the system device's cron on this pulse, or by a device's own triggered loop — same shape, same firing path in the shim, only the owner and location differ. Keeping the ground loop to just-the-beat is what makes that sameness hold. All ticket state stays on disk in the device's folder tree — greppable, no hidden runtime state.
invoke: A device subscribes by dropping a ticket-file into its own tree (its shim reads it on each pulse). The ground loop runs as one daemon and beats; on each beat it finds subscribers and pulses their shims. (Code is being reworked to this shape — see state.) Prove: `python3 cairn/ground_loop/proofs/test_ground_loop.py`.
node_class: device — but the MINIMAL one: the heartbeat daemon the whole loop-system rests on. It is not an executor; the executor/firing role belongs to the shim (that conflation was the goof this charter corrects).
entry: FRESH design to Form v0. The mechanism is Cairn's own: one daemon, one beat, subscription-by-file, firing-in-the-shim. No mechanism grafted.
state: REVISED 2026-07-18 — HYPOTHESIS/DESIGN, not yet proven in code (Law 3: labeled). The first cut (cairn `584aa74`) built the ground loop as a generic DRIVER-EXECUTOR (`run_driver`: resolve a proven method → run it → write a row to an owned target). That was the goof: the executor role, the ticket-firing, and the heartbeat got collapsed into one device, which lost the property that a ticket is the same unit no matter what fires it (Akien, 2026-07-18). The correction: the ground loop is ONLY the heartbeat; the resolve-and-fire logic moves into the SHIM's per-pulse ticket handling; tickets become on-disk files in each device's folder tree; the central-scheduler framing (system_rackmount) dissolves into this one universal mechanism. This intention is revised FIRST; the code (this component, the shim, system_rackmount) is reworked to match next. The proof will be rewritten for the heartbeat — the current `run_driver` proof proves the wrong shape.
gates: proof gate (tester) — proofs/test_ground_loop.py, runnable bare (python3) and under the tester. To be rewritten for the heartbeat shape. Durability gate: not done until proven AND committed.
falsifier: RED, by physics, on any of: (1) the ground loop does MORE than beat — if it resolves a method, runs a ticket, or routes a callback itself, it has re-absorbed the shim's role (the goof); (2) ticket-firing lives anywhere but the shim — a device that must implement its own loop to get its tickets fired breaks 'built into all devices'; (3) a device cannot subscribe simply by placing a ticket-file in its own folder tree (subscription must be a file the loop looks for, not a registration API); (4) a ticket fired by the system device's cron differs in shape or handling from one fired by a device's own loop (the sameness is the whole point); (5) ticket state is held in memory rather than on disk in the device's tree.
how_it_learns: The heartbeat itself does not learn — it is the floor everything beats on (like the core values, a deliberate terminal simplicity). Learning happens in the TICKETS the shims hang on it: each ticket is a handler whose firing leaves evidence on disk. The lesson this component itself carries is a specimen of learning-its-gates: collapsing the heartbeat, the executor, and the scheduler into one device was caught by Akien at n=1 and corrected before it hardened — the ground loop learned to be smaller. Its horizon: whether one heartbeat daemon + shim-fired tickets covers cron (system device) AND a device's own triggered loops with the SAME ticket — measured as the shim and the first real tickets land.
traces_to: Law 1 (one daemon, folded once — not re-derived as a bespoke loop per device)
Law 4 (a single heartbeat is physics; every handler hangs on it)
Law 5 (intent + code + proofs share this address)
Law 3 (this revision is a labeled hypothesis until the reworked code is proven)
the operational-driver primitive (a ticket = trigger(s) + what-to-call + why, resting on disk; MAP 'the operational-driver primitive')
cairn/base/shim.py (the shim carries the per-pulse ticket-firing — 'hang tickets on it' built into all devices)
CC memory [[cc-reify-vs-flow]] (the goof: three roles frozen into one device; the fix flows them apart)
build order: db domain → ground loop → … (MAP.md:892)
owner: CC owns the ground_loop code + its proofs and gates writes to them. The heartbeat is the shared substrate every device's shim depends on; the tickets hung on it are owned by their own devices, on disk in each device's tree.
proof: proofs/test_ground_loop.py — to be rewritten for the heartbeat shape (the beat pulses subscribed shims; a device subscribes by a file in its tree; the loop itself neither resolves nor runs a ticket). The current proof proves the superseded executor shape and will be replaced with the code rework.
provenance: Born 2026-07-18 as the fifth spine step, first built as a generic driver-executor (cairn `584aa74`). Same day, revised to the heartbeat after Akien restored the lost property — the ticket is the same unit whether fired by system cron or a device's own loop — and located the firing in the shim, subscription in a file in the device's tree, so 'hang tickets on the heartbeat' is built into every device. The revision SIMPLIFIES: the central scheduler (system_rackmount's scheduler service) dissolves, `run_driver` strips away, and one universal mechanism replaces three. Intention revised first; code rework (ground_loop → heartbeat, shim → ticket-firing, tickets → on-disk files) follows. Then the spine resumes (MAP.md:892): inference domain → web server.
